filter_index.video_title: Why We Love The Ali Bedni Bugyal Trek | A Tribute By Indiahikes

snow_or_rain_notes: March–April: lingering snow on higher slopes

terrain_challenges: Steep ascents and descents every day
Steep climb gaining nearly 3000 ft over 6 km on Day 1
Steep ascent before crossing the treeline at the onset of Bedni Bugyal on Day 3 (Gehroli Patal to Abin Kharak)
Neelganga River crossing can become risky in heavy rain
Day 1: ~3000 ft elevation gain over 6 km
altitude_or_weather_risks: Risk of Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) above 10,000 ft
Bedni Top at 12,550 ft; trek reaches 7,550 ft to 12,550 ft
AMS can affect first-time and experienced trekkers alike
Stay well-hydrated to lower AMS risk
Snowfall possible near Bedni Top, especially in winter

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 5 km in under 40 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
approval_requirements: Screenshots must demonstrate meeting the trek's fitness criteria
prep_window: Runs must meet trek's fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month

experience_themes: Spring: last patches of snow melting, bits lingering on higher slopes; forests wake with fresh green leaves; bright red rhododendrons bloom along the trail (especially near Gehroli Patal); thrushes and barbets call through the trees
Summer: lush greenery in meadows; wildflowers in abundance (best in June)
Post-monsoon: squeaky clean skies after monsoon clouds wash away
Winter: soft snow settles under oak and rhododendron boughs; west-facing slope catches early morning sun slanting through trees onto snow at magical angles
Spring: crisp, clean air; clear, sharp views of Mt. Trishul and Mt. Nanda Ghunti; snow-covered summits gleaming; ridge walk with Mt Trishul backdrop
Summer: green meadows with wildflowers; grazing horses with Mt Nanda Ghunti backdrop
primary_watchouts: Steep ascents and descents every day
Steep climb gaining nearly 3000 ft over 6 km on Day 1
Steep ascent before crossing the treeline at the onset of Bedni Bugyal on Day 3 (Gehroli Patal to Abin Kharak)
Neelganga River crossing can become risky in heavy rain
Day 1: ~3000 ft elevation gain over 6 km
Day 3: Steep ascent from Gehroli Patal to Abin Kharak before treeline
best_for: Summer (May–June): best overall time for lush greenery and wildflowers
Post-monsoon (September–November): best mountain views and photography of big mountains
Spring (March–April): among the best times for mountain views of Mt. Trishul and Mt. Nanda Ghunti

video_title: Why We Love The Ali Bedni Bugyal Trek | A Tribute By Indiahikes
